name: V1 nav: replace V0 family-capability inference profiles.some(!isOwner) with server hasFamilyLinks
description: Audit (ADR-register §6 AUDIT-C) found study/family V0 ships isFamilyCapableProfile = isOwner + adult + profiles.some(p => p.id!==active && !p.isOwner) in app-context.tsx - the exact inference the full study-and-family spec glossary explicitly forbids ('do not infer capability from profiles.some(!isOwner); use server hasFamilyLinks'). profiles.some(!isOwner) returns true for any non-owner profile (e.g. co-owner edge or managed-but-unlinked child), whereas family_links.parent_profile_id is a true parent->child edge. Benign on single-owner accounts today.
acceptanceCriteria: When V1 navigation contract ships: add server-sourced hasFamilyLinks:boolean to the profile response (computed from non-archived family_links rows) and have the client capability predicate consume it; do NOT carry forward profiles.some(!isOwner). Add a test asserting a non-owner-but-unlinked profile is NOT family-capable.
riskImpact: V1 upgrade-path landmine: if V1 persists the V0 array predicate instead of swapping to the server field, the forbidden inference becomes durable. Related to the tracked isAdultOwner null-guard bug (project_navcontract_isadultowner_null_bug, PR #595).
